ambiguity_dimensions.description: Clarify what support means: corridor, nearby teammate, receiving option, lane occupation, or another definition.
ambiguity_dimensions.label: support

ambiguity_dimensions.description: Clarify when support must arrive relative to possession, carry, pass, or line break.
ambiguity_dimensions.label: time window

ambiguity_dimensions.description: Clarify proximity language with an explicit distance threshold.
ambiguity_dimensions.label: distance threshold

agent_authority: search or describe recipes and capabilities
architecture_posture.future_mcp_transport: streamable HTTP or remote MCP after the local product path works
host_service_authority: result, trace, evidence, and replay retrieval
architecture_posture.mcp_role: Thin Hermes adapter over the host-owned application service; not a runtime, database, or permission layer.
architecture_posture.product_path: Hermes/frontier model authors or selects typed tactical plans; deterministic host validates, confirms, executes, and serves replay.

limitations: v0.1 supports adjacent same-team action pairs only.
Does not infer tactical intent, planned combination, or causation.

definition.description: Number of ordered actions in the chain. v0.1 supports adjacent pairs.
definition.name: chain_length

definition.description: Maximum elapsed provider event time between adjacent actions.
definition.name: maximum_action_gap_seconds

limitations: Uses provider events for action identity and nearest tracking frame for alignment.
Does not prove controlled reception, intent, optimality, or tactical role.

definition.description: Provider event action type to expose as anchors.
definition.name: action_type

limitations: Depends on canonical pitch coordinates and known pitch width.
Compares declared numeric evidence values only.
Does not infer why the change happened or whether it was tactically caused by the anchor event.

definition.description: After-record status field that must be PASS, or none to compare the numeric value without requiring after status.
definition.name: after_status_field

definition.description: After-record numeric evidence field to compare.
definition.name: after_value_field

definition.description: Before-record status field that must be PASS.
definition.name: before_status_field

definition.description: Before-record numeric evidence field to compare.
definition.name: before_value_field

definition.description: Direction of the required numeric change.
definition.name: change_mode

definition.description: Maximum allowed before value for compactness-style checks.
definition.name: maximum_before_value_m

definition.description: Minimum required change in metres.
definition.name: minimum_change_m

definition.description: Required before/after status value for numeric change evaluation.
definition.name: required_status_value

limitations: Requires a supplied observed line and exact anchor-correlated release/reception relative-position evidence.
Does not identify whether the supplied line is a first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive intent.

definition.description: Distance from the supplied line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
definition.name: line_buffer_m

limitations: Uses event data for candidate passes and tracking data for release/reception proof.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, or decision quality.

definition.description: Maximum ball-player distance for control evidence.
definition.name: control_distance_m

definition.description: Optional provider event-type filter for controlled-pass anchors.
definition.name: event_type_filter

definition.description: Minimum receiver-control dwell for controlled reception.
definition.name: minimum_receiver_dwell_seconds

definition.description: Margin allowing the intended player to count as controlling near teammates.
definition.name: nearest_teammate_margin_m

definition.description: Maximum release-to-controlled-reception search window.
definition.name: reception_search_seconds

definition.description: Search window after the event anchor for ball departure.
definition.name: release_search_after_seconds

definition.description: Search window before the event anchor for passer control.
definition.name: release_search_before_seconds

limitations: Models only an observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate the observed line.
definition.name: anchor_frame_field

definition.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
definition.name: goal_side_buffer_m

definition.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
definition.name: line_band_width_m

definition.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line.
definition.name: minimum_line_defenders

limitations: Goalkeepers are excluded from the outfield centroid.
No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No receiver body-orientation model.
No offside model in V1 acceptance.

limitations: Classifies observed outfield players only; it does not prove complete active-player lane coverage.
Uses fixed lateral lane geometry and does not infer player intention, support quality, optimality, or role semantics.
Does not prove a line break, controlled support arrival, or causal effect.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field used for lane evaluation.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Observed outfield player group to classify into lanes.
definition.name: player_scope

definition.description: Minimum number of observed lanes that must contain at least one selected player.
definition.name: required_occupied_lane_count

limitations: Observed geometric local count only; does not infer pressure or tactical role.
Does not prove support quality, player intention, causation, optimality, or pass probability.
Uses observed outfield tracking at the declared frame around the anchor reference point.

definition.description: Anchor record frame field where the local-number relation is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Maximum observed defending players inside the local region.
definition.name: maximum_defending_players

definition.description: Minimum perspective minus defending local player count.
definition.name: minimum_difference

definition.description: Minimum observed perspective players inside the local region.
definition.name: minimum_perspective_players

definition.description: Radius around the anchor reference point used for observed local counts.
definition.name: radius_m

limitations: Line ranks are observed geometric ordering only, not tactical first/second/midfield/back-line labels.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive role.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate observed line bands.
definition.name: anchor_frame_field

definition.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
definition.name: goal_side_buffer_m

definition.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
definition.name: line_band_width_m

definition.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line band.
definition.name: minimum_line_defenders

definition.description: Geometric goal-side line rank to expose for downstream relative-position tests.
definition.name: target_line_rank

limitations: Requires provider event linkage for v0.1; tracking-only redirection candidates are not modeled.
Claims only observed first-time relay under declared thresholds.
Does not infer tactical intention, third-man combination, pass optimality, or decision quality.

definition.description: Maximum observed relay touch-to-release duration for one-touch status.
definition.name: maximum_relay_dwell_seconds

definition.description: Maximum event-time gap from input pass event to relay pass event.
definition.name: relay_max_event_gap_seconds

definition.description: Maximum ball-player distance for relay-touch evidence.
definition.name: relay_touch_distance_m

limitations: Counts relative ball-position change between release and controlled reception.
Does not prove the pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.
Goalkeepers are excluded from the opponent denominator.

definition.description: Reception-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is behind the ball.
definition.name: bypassed_buffer_m

definition.description: Release-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is goal-side of the ball.
definition.name: goal_side_buffer_m

limitations: Settled possession is a frozen duration threshold, not a judgement of possession quality.
Does not infer intent, counterattack quality, or tactical causation.

definition.description: Maximum time after the anchor in which the settled threshold may be reached.
definition.name: maximum_window_seconds

definition.description: Continuous same-team active-ball possession duration required for SETTLED.
definition.name: minimum_settled_possession_seconds

definition.description: Optional upstream status field that must match before the window is evaluated.
definition.name: required_anchor_status_field

definition.description: Required upstream status value.
definition.name: required_anchor_status_value

limitations: Measures observed geometry only.
Does not infer pressure, marking quality, intent, or optimality.

definition.description: Anchor field containing the first entity ID.
definition.name: entity_a_field

definition.description: Anchor field containing the second entity ID, or ball.
definition.name: entity_b_field

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where distance is measured.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Threshold used for pairwise_distance_status.
definition.name: maximum_distance_m

limitations: Claims only connected event-linked receipt/relay/terminal controlled reception.
Does not infer planned combination, tactical intent, optimality, or pass probability.
Observed geometric/kinematic pressure only.
Does not infer pressure quality, defensive intent, scanning, decision quality, or causation.
Missing carrier or defender tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than false pressure.

definition.description: Candidate defender scope. v0.1 supports observed defending outfield players.
definition.name: candidate_scope

definition.description: Anchor-record field containing the pressured carrier id.
definition.name: carrier_id_field

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where pressure is evaluated.
definition.name: frame_field

definition.description: Velocity lookback interval for closing-speed and approach-angle estimates.
definition.name: lookback_seconds

definition.description: Maximum angle between defender velocity and defender-to-carrier vector.
definition.name: maximum_approach_angle_degrees

definition.description: Nearest-defender distance required for observed pressure.
definition.name: maximum_pressure_distance_m

definition.description: Minimum defender-to-carrier closing speed in metres per second.
definition.name: minimum_closing_speed_mps

definition.description: Minimum observed duration satisfying the pressure condition ending at the evaluation frame.
definition.name: minimum_pressure_duration_seconds

limitations: Measures receiver/relay-player position transition, not ball trajectory through a line.
Does not prove a tactical defensive-line break, causation, intention, or decision quality.
Measures entry into relation destination geometry only.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.

definition.description: Lookahead horizon for relation destination entry.

definition.name: episode_selection

definition.description: Seed used to derive deterministic relation-entry record IDs.

limitations: Classifies only observed position relative to a supplied geometric line.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.

definition.description: Frame field at which the entity position is evaluated.
definition.name: entity_frame_field

definition.description: Entity-reference field read from the entity anchor record.
definition.name: entity_id_field

definition.description: Distance from the line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
definition.name: line_buffer_m

limitations: Coarse zones only; not a tactical role or detailed positional-lane taxonomy.
Does not infer team intent, build-up phase quality, or territorial value.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where ball-zone membership is evaluated.

definition.description: Boundary buffer that turns near-boundary zone calls into UNKNOWN.

definition.description: Coarse attacking-direction-normalized pitch zone.

limitations: Observed geometric relation only; does not infer tactical intention.
Does not claim support quality, decision quality, communication, scanning, or optimality.
Uses observed outfield tracking around the declared anchor reference point.

definition.description: Anchor record frame field where support timing starts.

definition.description: Latest arrival after the support anchor frame.

definition.description: Minimum continuous observed duration inside the support region.

definition.description: Minimum number of observed candidates that must satisfy the support relation.

definition.description: Observed outfield team to measure.

limitations: Reports observed coordinate availability only.
Does not infer tracking accuracy beyond missing/usable coordinates in v0.1.

definition.description: Anchor-record frame field where quality is evaluated.

limitations: Uses the observed tracking possession-role stream as the transition source.

host_runtime_globals.name: maximum_analysis_gap_ms

recipe_contract.parameter_rule: default_invocation.parameters may only contain names declared in recipe.parameters or host-owned runtime globals. Catalog node parameters must use exact catalog parameter names and may be inline TypedValue objects or ParameterRef objects whose name appears in recipe.parameters.

capability_gap_codes.description: Request would alter primitive or relation definitions.
capability_gap_codes.label: primitive mutation

capability_gap_codes.description: Request asks the agent to execute directly.
capability_gap_codes.label: direct execution

capability_gap_codes.description: Tracking data cannot prove player intent.
capability_gap_codes.label: player intent

capability_gap_codes.description: No head-check or scanning primitive is available.
capability_gap_codes.label: scanning

capability_gap_codes.description: Optimal decision claims are out of scope.
capability_gap_codes.label: optimality

capability_gap_codes.description: Communication is not represented in tracking data.
capability_gap_codes.label: communication

capability_gap_codes.description: Video is outside the current data boundary.
capability_gap_codes.label: video

capability_gap_codes.description: Deception is not observable in current deterministic vocabulary.
capability_gap_codes.label: deception

capability_gap_codes.description: Facial cues are unavailable without video/perception.
capability_gap_codes.label: facial cues

allowed_claims_from_recipes: A controlled pass crossed a supplied observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared on the opposite side under the configured relation thresholds.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared under the configured relation thresholds.
Observed attacking players occupied at least the declared number of lateral lanes.
Observed local player counts satisfied the declared numeric relation.
Observed teammates satisfied the declared support-arrival relation around the controlled reception.
Replay/evidence comes from canonical event and tracking records.
The ball either entered or did not enter the selected corridor destination region within the configured horizon.
The ball entered a configured wide area.
The defending outfield centroid shifted toward the ball side by the configured threshold.
The event data identified a completed pass candidate.
The measured action bypassed the declared number of opposition outfield players.
The onward pass reached a terminal controlled reception.
The receiver/relay player transitioned from not beyond to beyond a supplied observed line.
The relay was event-linked and tracking-bounded under declared thresholds.
The replay coordinates come from canonical 25 Hz source frames.
The replay coordinates come from canonical source frames.
The replay coordinates come from canonical tracking frames.
The subsequent outcome was SWITCHED, RETAINED_NO_SWITCH, LOST_BEFORE_SWITCH, or excluded STOPPAGE under the frozen predicates.
The team had an active-ball possession anchor.
Tracking data confirmed physical release and controlled reception.
disallowed_claims_from_recipes: A pass should have been played.
A switch was always available.
Not switching was a mistake.
The attack intentionally caused the shift.
The attack intentionally created the corridor.
The corridor is optimal.
The decision was optimal or has an inferred completion probability.
The measured support was the right tactical option.
The moment proves an optimal decision or missed opportunity.
The pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.
The pass caused the line break or support response.
The pass or ball itself crossed the line.
The pass probability was high.
The pass was optimal.
The pass, support run, or resulting decision was optimal.
The passer intended to break a defensive line.
The players intended the measured outcome.
The result is a pass probability or video-backed claim.
The result is a pass probability, decision-quality, causation, player-intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
The result proves which defensive line was broken.
The sequence was a planned third-man combination.
The system identified a first, second, midfield, or back defensive line.
The system identified a tactical defensive-line role or definitive line break.
The system inferred player intent, communication, scanning, or pressure.
global_allowed_claims: The system can report deterministic measurements present in returned traces and evidence.
The system can say a typed plan matched or did not match according to PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN semantics.
The system can show coordinate replay from canonical IDSSE/DFL tracking data.
global_disallowed_claims: The system inferred player intent, body orientation, scanning, deception, or optimality.
The result is backed by match video.
The agent executed or confirmed a query without host authorization.

compare_query_versions.description: Compute deterministic semantic diffs for two query documents.

describe_capability.description: Describe one exposed tool, primitive, relation, operator, or generated typed-plan authoring contract.

execute_query_plan.description: Execute a validated plan through the deterministic runtime.

inspect_non_match.description: Evaluate a known timestamp target against a bound plan.

inspect_result.description: Return predicate traces and requested evidence for a result.

record_feedback.description: Append immutable analyst feedback for a result or known target.

retrieve_replay_window.description: Materialize a bounded coordinate replay JSON artifact.

save_experimental_recipe.description: Save a content-addressed experimental recipe version.

search_recipes.description: Search approved and experimental recipe summaries without exposing files or raw data.

submit_query_plan.description: Store a typed query document and return an opaque draft_plan_id.

validate_query_plan.description: Bind and boundary-check a submitted typed query plan.

pack_identity.purpose: Authoritative generated knowledge context for Hermes/frontier agents and human inspection.

primitives.name: possession_segment
primitives.purpose: Identify active-ball possession intervals for the perspective team.

limitations: Uses the observed tracking possession-role stream as the transition source.
Does not infer pressing trigger, transition intent, or tactical phase quality.
primitives.name: transition_anchor

parameters.description: Perspective-team possession boundary to expose.
parameters.name: transition_type

parameters.description: Minimum prior continuous possession by the other team before the transition is PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_prior_possession_seconds

parameters.description: Optional ball-zone filter evaluated at the transition frame.
parameters.name: zone_filter

parameters.description: Boundary buffer that turns near-boundary zone calls into UNKNOWN.
parameters.name: zone_boundary_buffer_m

primitives.purpose: Expose observed possession-change hinges for the perspective team from the tracking possession stream.

limitations: Coarse zones only; not a tactical role or detailed positional-lane taxonomy.
Does not infer team intent, build-up phase quality, or territorial value.
primitives.name: structured_zone

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where ball-zone membership is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Coarse attacking-direction-normalized pitch zone.
parameters.name: zone_name

parameters.description: Boundary buffer that turns near-boundary zone calls into UNKNOWN.
parameters.name: zone_boundary_buffer_m

primitives.purpose: Evaluate whether the ball is inside a declared coarse pitch zone at an anchor-relative frame.

limitations: Settled possession is a frozen duration threshold, not a judgement of possession quality.
Does not infer intent, counterattack quality, or tactical causation.
primitives.name: outcome_window

parameters.description: Maximum time after the anchor in which the settled threshold may be reached.
parameters.name: maximum_window_seconds

parameters.description: Continuous same-team active-ball possession duration required for SETTLED.
parameters.name: minimum_settled_possession_seconds

parameters.description: Optional upstream status field that must match before the window is evaluated.
parameters.name: required_anchor_status_field

parameters.description: Required upstream status value.
parameters.name: required_anchor_status_value

primitives.purpose: Evaluate whether same-team possession reaches a declared settled-duration threshold inside an anchor-relative window.

limitations: Uses provider events for action identity and nearest tracking frame for alignment.
Does not prove controlled reception, intent, optimality, or tactical role.
primitives.name: action_event_anchor

parameters.description: Provider event action type to expose as anchors.
parameters.name: action_type

primitives.purpose: Expose provider event rows as typed action anchors with frame alignment.

limitations: v0.1 supports adjacent same-team action pairs only.
Does not infer tactical intent, planned combination, or causation.
primitives.name: action_chain

parameters.description: Maximum elapsed provider event time between adjacent actions.
parameters.name: maximum_action_gap_seconds

parameters.description: Number of ordered actions in the chain. v0.1 supports adjacent pairs.
parameters.name: chain_length

primitives.purpose: Join ordered same-team action anchors into a bounded event-time action chain.

limitations: Reports observed coordinate availability only.
Does not infer tracking accuracy beyond missing/usable coordinates in v0.1.
primitives.name: tracking_quality

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where quality is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

primitives.purpose: Report whether ball and declared anchor entities have usable coordinates at an anchor-relative frame.

limitations: Measures observed geometry only.
Does not infer pressure, marking quality, intent, or optimality.
primitives.name: pairwise_distance

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where distance is measured.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Anchor field containing the first entity ID.
parameters.name: entity_a_field

parameters.description: Anchor field containing the second entity ID, or ball.
parameters.name: entity_b_field

parameters.description: Threshold used for pairwise_distance_status.
parameters.name: maximum_distance_m

primitives.purpose: Measure observed distance between an anchor entity and another entity or the ball at an anchor-relative frame.

limitations: Simple displacement-derived observed velocity only.
Does not infer effort, intent, role, or pressure quality.
Missing endpoint coordinates produce UNKNOWN.
primitives.name: velocity

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where velocity is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Anchor-record field containing the tracked player id.
parameters.name: entity_id_field

parameters.description: Time before the evaluation frame used for displacement velocity.
parameters.name: lookback_seconds

primitives.purpose: Estimate observed entity velocity around an anchor-relative frame from tracking displacement.

limitations: Uses observed outfield bounding-box width/depth only.
Does not infer tactical compactness quality, player intent, role assignment, or lane denial.
primitives.name: team_compactness

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where team compactness is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Observed outfield team to measure.
parameters.name: player_scope

parameters.description: Maximum lateral team width for compactness PASS.
parameters.name: maximum_team_width_m

parameters.description: Maximum longitudinal team depth for compactness PASS.
parameters.name: maximum_team_depth_m

parameters.description: Minimum observed outfield players required before compactness can be evaluated.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_players

primitives.purpose: Measure observed team width/depth at an anchor-relative frame and evaluate declared compactness thresholds.

limitations: Uses observed controlled-pass endpoints only.
Does not infer a designed switch, role taxonomy, tactical causation, or pass quality.
primitives.name: switch_of_play

parameters.description: Minimum absolute y-axis displacement from release to reception.
parameters.name: minimum_lateral_displacement_m

parameters.description: Minimum absolute release y-position for the pass to start wide enough.
parameters.name: minimum_start_lateral_m

parameters.description: Minimum absolute reception y-position for the pass to end wide enough.
parameters.name: minimum_end_lateral_m

parameters.description: Maximum release-to-reception duration for a switch candidate.
parameters.name: maximum_duration_seconds

primitives.purpose: Evaluate whether a controlled pass moved the ball from one lateral side to the opposite side under declared thresholds.

limitations: Compares declared numeric evidence values only.
Does not infer why the change happened or whether it was tactically caused by the anchor event.
primitives.name: change_across_anchor

parameters.description: Before-record numeric evidence field to compare.
parameters.name: before_value_field

parameters.description: After-record numeric evidence field to compare.
parameters.name: after_value_field

parameters.description: Before-record status field that must be PASS.
parameters.name: before_status_field

parameters.description: After-record status field that must be PASS, or none to compare the numeric value without requiring after status.
parameters.name: after_status_field

parameters.description: Required before/after status value for numeric change evaluation.
parameters.name: required_status_value

parameters.description: Direction of the required numeric change.
parameters.name: change_mode

parameters.description: Minimum required change in metres.
parameters.name: minimum_change_m

parameters.description: Maximum allowed before value for compactness-style checks.
parameters.name: maximum_before_value_m

primitives.purpose: Compare before and after numeric evidence values for the same anchor under declared change thresholds.

limitations: Uses event data for candidate passes and tracking data for release/reception proof.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, or decision quality.
primitives.name: controlled_pass_episode

parameters.description: Search window before the event anchor for passer control.
parameters.name: release_search_before_seconds

parameters.description: Search window after the event anchor for ball departure.
parameters.name: release_search_after_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum release-to-controlled-reception search window.
parameters.name: reception_search_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum ball-player distance for control evidence.
parameters.name: control_distance_m

parameters.description: Margin allowing the intended player to count as controlling near teammates.
parameters.name: nearest_teammate_margin_m

parameters.description: Minimum receiver-control dwell for controlled reception.
parameters.name: minimum_receiver_dwell_seconds

parameters.description: Optional provider event-type filter for controlled-pass anchors.
parameters.name: event_type_filter

primitives.purpose: Identify completed pass actions where event data supplies the candidate passer/receiver and tracking proves physical release plus controlled reception.

limitations: Requires provider event linkage for v0.1; tracking-only redirection candidates are not modeled.
Claims only observed first-time relay under declared thresholds.
Does not infer tactical intention, third-man combination, pass optimality, or decision quality.
primitives.name: one_touch_relay_episode

parameters.description: Maximum event-time gap from input pass event to relay pass event.
parameters.name: relay_max_event_gap_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum ball-player distance for relay-touch evidence.
parameters.name: relay_touch_distance_m

parameters.description: Maximum observed relay touch-to-release duration for one-touch status.
parameters.name: maximum_relay_dwell_seconds

primitives.purpose: Identify event-linked first-time relay candidates where a completed input pass is immediately followed by an onward completed pass by the input receiver, with tracking evidence for relay touch/release.

limitations: Models only an observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.
primitives.name: defensive_line_model

parameters.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
parameters.name: goal_side_buffer_m

parameters.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
parameters.name: line_band_width_m

parameters.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line.
parameters.name: minimum_line_defenders

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate the observed line.
parameters.name: anchor_frame_field

primitives.purpose: Fit an observed geometric defensive line at supplied anchor frames from defending outfield player tracking positions.

limitations: Line ranks are observed geometric ordering only, not tactical first/second/midfield/back-line labels.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive role.
primitives.name: multi_line_model

parameters.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
parameters.name: goal_side_buffer_m

parameters.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
parameters.name: line_band_width_m

parameters.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line band.
parameters.name: minimum_line_defenders

parameters.description: Geometric goal-side line rank to expose for downstream relative-position tests.
parameters.name: target_line_rank

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate observed line bands.
parameters.name: anchor_frame_field

primitives.purpose: Fit ranked observed defensive line bands at supplied anchor frames. Rank is geometric order goal-side of the ball, not tactical role taxonomy.

limitations: Classifies only observed position relative to a supplied geometric line.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.
primitives.name: relative_position_to_line

parameters.description: Entity-reference field read from the entity anchor record.
parameters.name: entity_id_field

parameters.description: Frame field at which the entity position is evaluated.
parameters.name: entity_frame_field

parameters.description: Distance from the line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
parameters.name: line_buffer_m

primitives.purpose: Classify an entity position as ahead of, behind, or level with an observed line at a declared frame.

limitations: Measures receiver/relay-player position transition, not ball trajectory through a line.
Does not prove a tactical defensive-line break, causation, intention, or decision quality.
primitives.name: receiver_line_transition_during_pass_leg
primitives.purpose: Evaluate whether the relay player moved from not beyond to beyond a supplied observed line between input-pass release and relay touch.

limitations: Claims only connected event-linked receipt/relay/terminal controlled reception.
Does not infer planned combination, tactical intent, optimality, or pass probability.
primitives.name: pass_chain_episode
primitives.purpose: Join an event-linked one-touch relay to a terminal controlled reception for the relay's onward pass.

limitations: Requires a supplied observed line and exact anchor-correlated release/reception relative-position evidence.
Does not identify whether the supplied line is a first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive intent.
primitives.name: controlled_line_break_episode

parameters.description: Distance from the supplied line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
parameters.name: line_buffer_m

primitives.purpose: Evaluate whether a controlled pass carried a receiver from not-yet-beyond a supplied observed line at release to beyond that same line at controlled reception.

limitations: Classifies observed outfield players only; it does not prove complete active-player lane coverage.
Uses fixed lateral lane geometry and does not infer player intention, support quality, optimality, or role semantics.
Does not prove a line break, controlled support arrival, or causal effect.
primitives.name: lane_occupancy

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field used for lane evaluation.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Observed outfield player group to classify into lanes.
parameters.name: player_scope

parameters.description: Minimum number of observed lanes that must contain at least one selected player.
parameters.name: required_occupied_lane_count

primitives.purpose: Classify observed outfield players into lateral pitch lanes at an anchor-relative frame and evaluate declared lane-count requirements.

limitations: Depends on canonical pitch coordinates and known pitch width.
primitives.name: ball_lateral_fraction
primitives.purpose: Normalize ball distance from pitch centerline toward either touchline.

limitations: Goalkeepers are excluded from the outfield centroid.
primitives.name: defensive_outfield_centroid
primitives.purpose: Measure defending outfield team centroid without goalkeeper bias.

primitives.name: signed_lateral_shift
primitives.purpose: Measure defensive centroid movement toward the ball side.

limitations: Does not infer intent, optimality, or missed opportunity.
primitives.name: outcome_classification

parameters.description: Outcome lookahead horizon in seconds.
parameters.name: horizon

primitives.purpose: Classify the post-anchor outcome under the frozen M1 predicates.

safe_generic_path.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
safe_generic_path.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

limitations: Measures entry into relation destination geometry only.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
primitives.name: relation_destination_entry

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for relation destination entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic relation-entry record IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic witness ordering when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

primitives.purpose: Measure whether the ball enters the exact destination region of a relation episode inside a declared post-open window.

agent_authoring.reason: Recipe-specific wrapper; agent-authored plans must use the generic entry_status path.

limitations: Trusted recipe wrapper; agent-authored plans should use relation_destination_entry.
Requires an upstream relation episode set.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
primitives.name: relation_destination_entry_classification

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for destination lane entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic experimental result IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic selection strategy when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

primitives.purpose: Classify whether the ball later enters the destination region of a previously evaluated relation episode.

schema.title: Tactical Query IR v1 Schema Bundle

allowed_claims: The ball entered a configured wide area.
The defending outfield centroid shifted toward the ball side by the configured threshold.
The subsequent outcome was SWITCHED, RETAINED_NO_SWITCH, LOST_BEFORE_SWITCH, or excluded STOPPAGE under the frozen predicates.
The replay coordinates come from canonical 25 Hz source frames.

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 switched result classification.
classification_rules.label: SWITCHED

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 retained-no-switch result classification.
classification_rules.label: RETAINED_NO_SWITCH

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 loss-before-switch result classification.
classification_rules.label: LOST_BEFORE_SWITCH

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 excluded stoppage classification.
classification_rules.label: STOPPAGE

constraints: The authored plan must keep status=experimental for Hermes.
Keep default_invocation.execution_mode=execute; host confirmation still controls whether execution may happen.
exists/count_at_least may only consume anchor_evaluations.
Use relation_destination_entry.entry_status with eq PASS for agent-authored destination-region entry checks.
Do not use trusted recipe wrappers such as relation_destination_entry_classification in agent-authored plans.
Do not request raw match dumps, primitive mutation, confirmation, or execution.

default_invocation_contract.validation_contract: validate_query_plan binds and checks the plan but never executes it.

destination_entry_after_possession_corri.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
destination_entry_after_possession_corri.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

recipes.description: Find possessions where the ball enters a wide channel and the defending outfield block shifts toward that side under the frozen M1 semantics.
disallowed_claims: The attack intentionally caused the shift.
A switch was always available.
Not switching was a mistake.
The moment proves an optimal decision or missed opportunity.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
limitations: M1.1 Gate A binds this plan only; execution and parity are Gate B.
The legacy M1 detector remains the read-only oracle through the demo.
No pass probability, optimality, or player-intent claim is allowed.

parameters.description: Frozen M1 query hash used only to preserve legacy result IDs during parity.
parameters.name: result_id_seed_hash

parameters.description: Analysis cadence used by the frozen M1 detector.
parameters.name: analysis_rate_hz

parameters.description: Minimum active-ball possession duration.
parameters.name: minimum_possession_seconds

parameters.description: Ball lateral fraction required for wide entry.
parameters.name: wide_entry_fraction

parameters.description: Prior central-ball fraction required before a wide entry.
parameters.name: prior_central_fraction

parameters.description: Minimum time the ball must dwell in the wide channel.
parameters.name: minimum_wide_dwell_seconds

parameters.description: Pre-entry baseline window for defensive centroid measurement.
parameters.name: baseline_window_seconds

parameters.description: Post-entry search window for the maximum signed defensive shift.
parameters.name: shift_search_window_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum signed defending block shift toward the ball side.
parameters.name: minimum_shift_metres

parameters.description: Minimum time the block shift must persist.
parameters.name: minimum_shift_persistence_seconds

parameters.description: Post-anchor horizon used for outcome classification.
parameters.name: outcome_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Opposite-side ball lateral fraction used for SWITCHED classification.
parameters.name: opposite_side_fraction

parameters.description: Minimum retained-possession time after opposite-side entry.
parameters.name: retained_after_switch_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum permitted gap in the 5 Hz analysis stream.
parameters.name: maximum_analysis_gap_ms

parameters.description: Minimum defending outfield players required for accepted quality.
parameters.name: minimum_outfield_players_per_team

parameters.description: Minimum source-frame separation between accepted wide-entry candidates.
parameters.name: dedupe_window_seconds

parameters.description: Frozen proof result limit from M1.
parameters.name: accepted_result_limit

parameters.description: Frozen proof selection cap per evaluation match.
parameters.name: accepted_per_match_limit

parameters.description: Frozen selected near-miss cap.
parameters.name: near_miss_limit

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 switched result classification.
classification_rules.label: SWITCHED

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 retained-no-switch result classification.
classification_rules.label: RETAINED_NO_SWITCH

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 loss-before-switch result classification.
classification_rules.label: LOST_BEFORE_SWITCH

classification_rules.description: Frozen M1 excluded stoppage classification.
classification_rules.label: STOPPAGE

allowed_claims: The team had an active-ball possession anchor.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared under the configured relation thresholds.
The replay coordinates come from canonical 25 Hz source frames.

classification_rules.description: A geometric progressive corridor appeared from a generic possession anchor.
classification_rules.label: PROGRESSIVE_CORRIDOR_AVAILABLE

constraints: The authored plan must keep status=experimental for Hermes.
Keep default_invocation.execution_mode=execute; host confirmation still controls whether execution may happen.
exists/count_at_least may only consume anchor_evaluations.
Use relation_destination_entry.entry_status with eq PASS for agent-authored destination-region entry checks.
Do not use trusted recipe wrappers such as relation_destination_entry_classification in agent-authored plans.
Do not request raw match dumps, primitive mutation, confirmation, or execution.

default_invocation_contract.validation_contract: validate_query_plan binds and checks the plan but never executes it.

destination_entry_after_possession_corri.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
destination_entry_after_possession_corri.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

recipes.description: Experimental composition that starts from ordinary possession anchors and detects whether a geometric progressive corridor appears without using the M1 wide-entry block-shift spine.
disallowed_claims: The attack intentionally created the corridor.
A pass should have been played.
The corridor is optimal.
The result is a pass probability, decision-quality, causation, player-intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
No offside, receiver body orientation, pressure, pass probability, or optimality model is evaluated.
No match video is used.

parameters.description: Analysis cadence.
parameters.name: analysis_rate_hz

parameters.description: Minimum active-ball possession duration.
parameters.name: minimum_possession_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum permitted gap in the analysis stream.
parameters.name: maximum_analysis_gap_ms

parameters.description: Minimum outfield players required per team for quality checks.
parameters.name: minimum_outfield_players_per_team

parameters.description: Maximum post-anchor relation search window.
parameters.name: corridor_max_window_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum forward progression for a corridor segment.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_progression_m

parameters.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: corridor_maximum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Minimum defender clearance from the corridor segment.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_clearance_m

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
parameters.name: corridor_open_after_frames

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
parameters.name: corridor_close_after_frames

parameters.description: Minimum open duration retained in relation output.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_duration_seconds

classification_rules.description: A geometric progressive corridor appeared from a generic possession anchor.
classification_rules.label: PROGRESSIVE_CORRIDOR_AVAILABLE

allowed_claims: The ball entered a configured wide area.
The defending outfield centroid shifted toward the ball side by the configured threshold.
A geometric progressive corridor appeared on the opposite side under the configured relation thresholds.
The ball either entered or did not enter the selected corridor destination region within the configured horizon.
The replay coordinates come from canonical 25 Hz source frames.

classification_rules.description: The opposite-side geometric progressive corridor persisted and the ball entered the selected destination region.
classification_rules.label: DESTINATION_ENTERED

classification_rules.description: The opposite-side geometric progressive corridor persisted, but the ball did not enter the selected destination region within the horizon.
classification_rules.label: CORRIDOR_PERSISTED_NO_DESTINATION_ENTRY

constraints: The authored plan must keep status=experimental for Hermes.
Keep default_invocation.execution_mode=execute; host confirmation still controls whether execution may happen.
exists/count_at_least may only consume anchor_evaluations.
Use relation_destination_entry.entry_status with eq PASS for agent-authored destination-region entry checks.
Do not use trusted recipe wrappers such as relation_destination_entry_classification in agent-authored plans.
Do not request raw match dumps, primitive mutation, confirmation, or execution.

default_invocation_contract.validation_contract: validate_query_plan binds and checks the plan but never executes it.

destination_entry_after_possession_corri.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
destination_entry_after_possession_corri.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

recipes.description: Experimental composition that finds wide-entry block shifts, evaluates opposite-side geometric progressive corridors, and classifies whether the ball enters the corridor destination region.
disallowed_claims: The attack intentionally created the corridor.
A pass should have been played.
The corridor is optimal.
The result is a pass probability, decision-quality, causation, player-intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
Destination entry is geometric ball-region entry only.
No offside, receiver body orientation, pressure, pass probability, or optimality model is evaluated.
No match video is used.

parameters.description: Prior central-ball fraction required before wide entry.
parameters.name: prior_central_fraction

parameters.name: minimum_wide_dwell_seconds

parameters.name: minimum_shift_persistence_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum corridor segment length.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Maximum corridor segment length.
parameters.name: corridor_maximum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Minimum defender clearance from the corridor segment.
parameters.name: corridor_minimum_clearance_m

parameters.description: Consecutive passing relation frames required to open the corridor.
parameters.name: corridor_open_after_frames

parameters.description: Consecutive failing relation frames required to close the corridor.
parameters.name: corridor_close_after_frames

classification_rules.description: The opposite-side geometric progressive corridor persisted and the ball entered the selected destination region.
classification_rules.label: DESTINATION_ENTERED

classification_rules.description: The opposite-side geometric progressive corridor persisted, but the ball did not enter the selected destination region within the horizon.
classification_rules.label: CORRIDOR_PERSISTED_NO_DESTINATION_ENTRY

allowed_claims: The event data identified a completed pass candidate.
Tracking data confirmed physical release and controlled reception.
The measured action bypassed the declared number of opposition outfield players.
The replay coordinates come from canonical source frames.

classification_rules.description: A completed controlled pass progressed the ball and bypassed at least the declared number of opposition outfield players.
classification_rules.label: HIGH_BYPASS_COMPLETED_PASS

constraints: The authored plan must keep status=experimental for Hermes.
Keep default_invocation.execution_mode=execute; host confirmation still controls whether execution may happen.
exists/count_at_least may only consume anchor_evaluations.
Use relation_destination_entry.entry_status with eq PASS for agent-authored destination-region entry checks.
Do not use trusted recipe wrappers such as relation_destination_entry_classification in agent-authored plans.
Do not request raw match dumps, primitive mutation, confirmation, or execution.

destination_entry_after_possession_corri.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
destination_entry_after_possession_corri.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

recipes.description: Experimental composition that finds completed controlled passes where the ball progresses forward and at least a declared number of opposition outfield players move from goal-side of the ball at release to behind the ball at controlled reception.
disallowed_claims: The pass was optimal.
The pass probability was high.
The passer intended to break a defensive line.
The pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.
The result proves which defensive line was broken.
The result is backed by licensed match video.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
Bypass count is measured relative to ball position at release and controlled reception.
Goalkeepers are excluded from the opposition denominator.
No defensive-line model, offside model, pass probability, body-orientation model, or intent model is evaluated.

classification_rules.description: A completed controlled pass progressed the ball and bypassed at least the declared number of opposition outfield players.
classification_rules.label: HIGH_BYPASS_COMPLETED_PASS

allowed_claims: A controlled pass crossed a supplied observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Observed teammates satisfied the declared support-arrival relation around the controlled reception.
Observed attacking players occupied at least the declared number of lateral lanes.
Observed local player counts satisfied the declared numeric relation.
The replay coordinates come from canonical tracking frames.

classification_rules.description: Observed controlled line crossing with declared support arrival, lane occupancy, and local-number context.
classification_rules.label: LINE_BREAK_SUPPORT_RESPONSE

constraints: The authored plan must keep status=experimental for Hermes.

destination_entry_after_possession_corri.claims_boundary: Measures geometric ball entry into a relation destination region only; no pass probability, optimality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
destination_entry_after_possession_corri.purpose: Agent-authorable generic composition for possession-start progressive corridors followed by ball entry into the relation destination region.

recipes.description: Experimental capstone composition that finds observed controlled-pass crossings of a supplied geometric defensive line and evaluates the declared support-arrival, lane-occupancy, and local-number context around the controlled reception.
disallowed_claims: The system identified a first, second, midfield, or back defensive line.
The pass caused the line break or support response.
The pass, support run, or resulting decision was optimal.
The measured support was the right tactical option.
The system inferred player intent, communication, scanning, or pressure.
The result is a pass probability or video-backed claim.
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
Line crossing uses a supplied observed geometric line, not a named tactical line taxonomy.
Support, lane, and local-number outputs are observed geometric relations only.
No offside, pressure, body orientation, pass probability, decision-quality, or intention model is evaluated.

recipes.description: Experimental composition that finds event-linked first-time relays where the receiver/relay player moves beyond a supplied observed line during the input pass leg and the onward pass has a terminal controlled reception.
disallowed_claims: The pass or ball itself crossed the line.
The system identified a tactical defensive-line role or definitive line break.
The sequence was a planned third-man combination.
The players intended the measured outcome.
The decision was optimal or has an inferred completion probability.
recipes.display_name: First-Time Relay After Receiver Line Transition
limitations: Experimental plan; not an approved recipe.
v0.1 requires provider event linkage for the relay.
Tracking-only redirection candidates are deferred.
The line-transition measurement is a receiver/relay-player position transition, not ball trajectory.
